# Dominatus.Actuators.Standard M3 — Local Calendar `.ics` Actuators

## Purpose

M3 adds **typed, local, sandboxed** iCalendar (`.ics`) event writing for `Dominatus.Actuators.Standard`.

This milestone is intentionally boring:

- local filesystem only
- no network calls
- no account login or API keys
- no cloud calendar integrations

## What M3 includes

- `WriteCalendarEventCommand` for writing/replacing a full `VCALENDAR` file with one `VEVENT`
- `AppendCalendarEventCommand` for conservatively appending a `VEVENT` before `END:VCALENDAR`
- `CalendarEventSpec` with UID/title/start/end and optional description/location/reminder
- `CalendarReminderSpec` for display alarm before event start
- `CalendarWriteResult` typed completion payload
- `RegisterStandardCalendarActuators(...)` host registration helper

## What M3 explicitly does not include

- Google Calendar / Outlook / CalDAV
- OAuth or any login flow
- SMTP invites / attendees / RSVP workflows
- schedulers, cron, execute-later, background jobs
- ICS import parser
- recurrence engine
- timezone database
- VTODO/task support

## Public API

```csharp
var options = new CalendarActuatorOptions
{
    Roots =
    [
        new SandboxedFileRoot("calendar", @"C:\\Users\\me\\Calendars")
    ]
};

var host = new ActuatorHost();
host.RegisterStandardCalendarActuators(options, fakeClock);

yield return Ai.Act(
    new WriteCalendarEventCommand(
        Root: "calendar",
        Path: "reminders/team-sync.ics",
        Event: new CalendarEventSpec(
            Uid: "team-sync-2026-05-04@dominatus.local",
            Title: "Team sync",
            Start: new DateTimeOffset(2026, 5, 4, 17, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero),
            End: new DateTimeOffset(2026, 5, 4, 17, 30, 0, TimeSpan.Zero),
            Description: "Generated by Dominatus.",
            Location: "Online",
            Reminder: new CalendarReminderSpec(TimeSpan.FromMinutes(15))),
        Overwrite: true),
    Keys.CalendarWriteId);
```

> Use `Ai.Act` from `Dominatus.OptFlow` in authoring projects; `Dominatus.Actuators.Standard` itself does not depend on OptFlow.

## Sandbox root/path policy

- Commands take `Root` + relative `Path`
- Absolute paths are rejected
- Path traversal is rejected
- Resolved paths must remain under configured allowlisted roots
- `.ics` extension is required
- Parent directories are created only within the sandbox root

## Date/time policy

- API inputs use `DateTimeOffset`
- Emitted ICS uses UTC timestamps (`...Z`)
- `DTSTAMP` comes from `ICalendarSystemClock` for deterministic tests

## Write vs append behavior

- **Write**
  - emits a complete `VCALENDAR` with one `VEVENT`
  - fails if file exists and `Overwrite` is `false`
  - replaces file when `Overwrite` is `true`
- **Append**
  - creates new `VCALENDAR` when file does not exist
  - appends `VEVENT` before `END:VCALENDAR` when file exists
  - fails on malformed/unrecognized existing calendar payload
  - does not do full ICS parsing

## ICS writer behavior

- Writes iCalendar 2.0 skeleton with:
  - `BEGIN:VCALENDAR`
  - `VERSION:2.0`
  - `PRODID:-//Dominatus//Dominatus.Actuators.Standard//EN`
  - `CALSCALE:GREGORIAN`
  - `VEVENT` body with UID/DTSTAMP/DTSTART/DTEND/SUMMARY
- Optional fields only when provided: `DESCRIPTION`, `LOCATION`
- Optional `VALARM` with display reminder
- Escapes text (`\\`, `;`, `,`, newlines)
- Folds long lines at 75 characters (simple character-based folding)

## Size and safety caps

`CalendarActuatorOptions` supports:

- `MaxCalendarBytes` (total file size)
- `MaxEventBytes` (single rendered event payload size)

Both limits are enforced during write/append.
